The '''Pitrillistians''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of large, imposing beings hailing from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pitrillistia]], in the [[Mieru'kar sector/Legends|Mieru'kar sector]]. They were a peaceful people who communicated through a series of [[Music/Legends|musical]] buzzes. The Pitrillistians were marked by the fact that they had two completely different fine [[Limb/Legends|manipulators]]—the left was more [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]], while the right was more [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]].

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Pitrillistian_front_view.png|thumb|upright|left|Pitrillistians stood seven feet tall.]]
The Pitrillistians were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] who stood seven [[Foot/Legends|feet]] tall and had [[Color/Legends|purple]], two-piece shells. Under these carapaces, they had small wings that allowed them to communicate. They used their proboscises to extract [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]] from fluids. The [[Species/Legends|species]]' anatomy featured two completely different fine [[Limb/Legends|manipulators]]. The left was more [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] in nature, sporting two long pincers. The right, however, boasted a more [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] hand with four fingers, which allowed them to wield [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and the like. Their feet ended in sharp claws. The Pitrillistian face was red, with green [[Hair/Legends|hair]] along the snout. On either side of the head were two large bluish-white horns, which hung down to the individual's hips. Between their shells was a large tuft of brown fur, and on either wrist was a similar tuft of white fur.<ref name="daa" />

==Society and culture==
Pitrillistians wore brightly-colored clothing, which made them an imposing sight to other sentients. They were a peaceful people, and spoke in a [[Pitrillistian language|language]] that consisted mainly of [[Music/Legends|musical]] buzzes created by their wings. Pitrillistians could wield blasters and carry [[Staff/Legends|staffs]].<ref name="daa" />

==History==
The Pitrillistians were native to [[Pitrillistia]], a [[Planet/Legends|world]] located on the edge of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] border,<ref name="daa" /> in the [[Mieru'kar sector/Legends|Mieru'kar sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="tea">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the species' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] had fallen into territory controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] faction known as [[Oversector Outer]]; however, the region was largely neutral by [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].<ref name="TEA 198">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Pitrillistians were created by [[Desset Fabien]] through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition run in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine. The competition, launched in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]), called for readers to create completely new [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species inspired by the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' films. Fabien was nineteen years of age when he submitted his entry, which was later published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]) under the "honorable mentions" section of the "16 and over" subdivision. Continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] later stated that published material from the "Design an Alien" competition was considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the "Design an Alien" contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> The planet Pitrillistia, which was created by Fabien in his submission, was later included in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ([[2009]]), written by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]].
